differentials.name: Allantoic cyst =  remnant of umbilical vesicle / allantois; usually degenerates by 6 weeks
differentials.raw: Allantoic cyst =  remnant of umbilical vesicle / allantois; usually degenerates by 6 weeks

differentials.name: Amniotic inclusion cyst =  amniotic epithelium trapped within umbilical cord
differentials.raw: Amniotic inclusion cyst =  amniotic epithelium trapped within umbilical cord

TRUE UMBILICAL CORD CYST: focal thickening of Wharton jelly Prognosis:  usually resolved by 12 weeks MA Prevalence:  3.4% of 1st trimester pregnancies; 20% persist into 2nd trimester Histo:   lined by single layer of flattened epithelium Rarely associated with:   fetal structural anomaly, aneuploidy Size:  4-60 mm in diameter Site:   eccentric in cord Site:   in center of cord
